methods.description: Process for preparing ice core sawdust samples for 10Be analysis, including cleaning, carrier addition, and loading into ion exchange columns.

steps.description: The collection equipment was thoroughly cleaned between each sampling event using a brush to reduce cross contamination as much as possible.
steps.searchable_summary: clean sampling equipment to prevent cross contamination

steps.description: Before melting the ice sample, 100 microgram of 9Be-carrier was added.
The water was, without filtration, loaded into disposable ion exchange columns.
steps.searchable_summary: load water into ion exchange columns

methods.description: Procedure for extracting 10Be and 9Be from samples, precipitating Be-hydroxide, and preparing samples for AMS measurements.

steps.description: The 10Be and 9Be were extracted from the column using 4 M HCl and Be-hydroxide was precipitated using NH3 and then left overnight.
steps.searchable_summary: extract Be isotopes and precipitate Be-hydroxide

steps.description: The beryllium-hydroxide reduced to BeO by heating to a temperature of 600°C at a final yield of .85%.
steps.searchable_summary: convert Be-hydroxide to BeO by heating

steps.description: Nb-powder was mixed with the BeO and the mixture was pressed into target for AMS measurements.
steps.searchable_summary: mix Nb-powder with BeO and prepare AMS target

methods.description: Measurement of 10Be concentrations using AMS, including sample cycling and interference reduction.

steps.description: The Uppsala University AMS system was used for the measurement at a terminal voltage of 4.7 MV using 40 sample MC-SNICS (NEC) ion source and ion-beam current at 0.5-1 mA.
steps.searchable_summary: perform AMS measurement at specified voltage and current

steps.description: Each sample was measured for three cycles for up to 20 minutes per sample.
steps.searchable_summary: measure each sample in three cycles

steps.description: Isobaric interference from 10B was additionally reduced one order of magnitude through use of a second stripper foil and a vertical 180° dipole magnet installed before the detector system.
steps.searchable_summary: reduce 10B interference using stripper foil and dipole magnet

methods.description: Calculation of 10Be concentrations using NIST SRM 4325 standard and error analysis.

steps.description: The concentration of 10Be were calculated using the NIST SRM 4325 standard at machine and statistical errors <15%.

Although the mean average error of the samples is around 5.6%, the variability from one sample to another is related to variability in counting rates that is affected by sample current and amount of Nb-BeO mixture remaining in the cathode.
